title: 10 best herbal tinctures for morning sickness
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for morning sickness are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate nausea and vomiting during pregnancy. These tinctures help by targeting various physiological mechanisms that contribute to morning sickness, providing relief and comfort to expectant mothers. The best herbal tinctures for morning sickness include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Avena sativa, Cinchona officinalis, Angelica archangelica, Piper methysticum, Foeniculum vulgare, Silybum marianum, and Mentha x piperita. Without effective treatment, morning sickness can lead to dehydration, weight loss, and fatigue, significantly impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which ease digestive discomfort and alleviate nausea. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to calm stomach muscles and reduce inflammation in the gut, helping to stabilize blood sugar levels and soothe an upset stomach. By targeting these root causes of morning sickness, herbal ginger tinctures provide a natural remedy that can help alleviate symptoms and provide relief from the discomfort associated with pregnancy.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant property: Ginger tincture helps to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea by increasing gastric motility, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, and inhibiting the production of stomach acid.
Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to morning sickness symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Gastroprotective property: Ginger tincture has been shown to have gastroprotective effects by reducing gastric ulcers and protecting the mucous membranes of the stomach and intestines from damage, helping to alleviate symptoms of morning sickness.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.
Shogaols: These vanillyl compounds are responsible for ginger's anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea effects, which can help reduce morning sickness symptoms.
Zingerone: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its ability to alleviate morning sickness by reducing nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties that help alleviate morning sickness.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are known for their ability to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.
Buds: Ginger buds contain high levels of gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of ginger, including relief from morning sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale by gently digging them up from the ground.
Cut the harvested rhizomes into small pieces and weigh them to ensure the desired dosage of 1:3.
Combine the rhizome pieces with 3 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container and stir well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking to speed up the process.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its natural ability to calm the digestive system. The menthol in peppermint has a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and alleviating nausea. Additionally, peppermint's carminative properties help to relieve bloating and cramps, further reducing symptoms of morning sickness. By promoting digestion and relaxation, herbal peppermint tinctures can provide quick relief from the uncomfortable and debilitating effects of morning sickness during pregnancy.
remedy_properties: Digestive relaxant: Peppermint tincture's antispasmodic properties help to ease digestive discomfort, nausea, and vomiting associated with morning sickness by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines.

Anti-inflammatory: The menthol content in peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, further alleviating symptoms of morning sickness.

Anxiolytic: Peppermint's calming and soothing properties have a mild anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbate morning sickness, promoting a sense of calm and relief.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps alleviate morning sickness by reducing nausea and vomiting through its local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects on the stomach and digestive tract.
Menthone: Menthone exhibits anti-emetic properties, which help to suppress the vomiting reflex, thereby providing relief from morning sickness.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce the severity of morning sickness by mitigating oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are used to make tinctures for morning sickness because of their high concentration of menthol and other essential oils that provide relief from nausea and vomiting.
Flowers: The flowers of Mentha x piperita are used to make tinctures for morning sickness due to their mild, carminative properties that help alleviate digestive discomfort and reduce nausea.

Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to make tinctures for morning sickness because they contain menthol and other compounds that help to relax the digestive system and reduce morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material to 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small glass bottles and label them with the date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Raspberry tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its unique combination of flavonoids, vitamins, and minerals that work in harmony to alleviate nausea and vomiting. The tannins present in raspberry leaf help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, while the vitamin C and magnesium content work to calm the stomach and regulate digestion. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of raspberry tincture can help to ease muscle cramps and spasms that often accompany morning sickness.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C content: Raspberry tincture's high vitamin C content helps to combat nausea and vomiting by supporting immune function and reducing inflammation in the body.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Raspberry tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like ellagic acid and anthocyanins, which can help alleviate morning sickness symptoms by soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Raspberry tincture's rich antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to morning sickness and other pregnancy-related symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help alleviate morning sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with nausea and vomiting during pregnancy.
Ellagic acid: A polyphenolic compound found in Rubus idaeus, ellagic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties, helping to alleviate morning sickness symptoms.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, have been found to possess anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate morning sickness and other pregnancy-related discomforts.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
Fruits: The ripe fruits are high in vitamins, minerals, and anthocyanins, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Stems: The stems, particularly the bark, contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which may help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh rubus idaeus leaves and 2 parts of fresh rubus idaeus stems for tincture.
Chop the gathered rubus idaeus into small pieces for easier extraction of medicinal compounds.
Combine the chopped rubus idaeus with 60 percent 95 grain ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks or 30 days.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or fine mesh to separate the plant material from the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and ease stomach discomfort. The herb's gentle calming effects also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of morning sickness. Additionally, chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by relaxing the uterine muscles and improving digestion. As a result, chamomile tinctures provide a natural relief from morning sickness symptoms, allowing expectant mothers to feel more comfortable and at ease throughout their pregnancy journey.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile tincture's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common contributors to morning sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract, which can exacerbate morning sickness symptoms like nausea and vomiting.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue can help soothe the digestive system and alleviate spasms that can contribute to morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the nausea and cramping associated with morning sickness.
Luteolin: Luteolin, another flavonoid present in Matricaria chamomilla, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce symptoms of morning sickness.

Bisabolol: Bisabolol, a sesquiterpene alcohol found in Matricaria chamomilla, is known for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe the stomach and alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for morning sickness due to their high concentration of apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for morning sickness because they contain apigenin and other flavonoids that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for morning sickness due to their high concentration of apigenin and other flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for morning sickness due to their high concentration of apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for morning sickness due to their high concentration of apigenin and other flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for morning sickness because they contain apigenin and other flavonoids that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them using paper bags or a dehydrator for 1 to 2 hours at 95 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1 part of the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to ensure the extraction process is efficient and effective.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the resulting tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, using 20 to 30 drops per dose as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its calming and digestive properties. The natural essential oils in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting. Additionally, lemon balm's ability to relax the muscles and calm the mind can ease anxiety and stress, which are often contributors to morning sickness. By using a herbal lemon balm tincture, women may find relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of morning sickness, allowing them to feel more comfortable and refreshed throughout their day.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can calm digestive irritation and alleviate nausea, helping to reduce morning sickness symptoms.
Antispasmodic Effects: The volatile oils in lemon balm, like citral and geraniol, have a calming effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with morning sickness.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Lemon balm's flavonoids and terpenes can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common exacerbators of morning sickness, allowing for a more restful and comfortable experience during pregnancy.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that helps alleviate morning sickness by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting.

Linalool: A terpene that has been shown to have a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety related to morning sickness, ultimately leading to a decrease in nausea and vomiting.
Limonene: A terpene that has been found to have anti-nausea and anti-emetic properties, which can help alleviate morning sickness by reducing the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of rosmarinic acid, which is believed to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.
Flowers: They are known for their calming properties and contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may help soothe digestive issues and reduce morning sickness symptoms.
Stems: They contain similar compounds to the leaves and flowers, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which may help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning to ensure highest concentration of essential oils.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Chop the dried melissa officinalis into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 60% vodka and 40% distilled water in a 1:5 ratio to create tincture solution.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid for 2 weeks in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its natural ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate nausea, and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The herb's active compounds, such as fenchone and anethole, work to calm the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate morning sickness symptoms. Additionally, fennel tincture's carminative properties help to release trapped gas, further alleviating discomfort and nausea, making it a popular natural remedy for expectant mothers seeking relief from morning sickness.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Fennel's natural oils, such as anethole, stimulate digestive enzymes and alleviate nausea and vomiting by promoting regular bowel movements.
Antispasmodic: Fennel's active compounds, like flavonoids and terpenes, relax the uterine muscles and reduce spasms in the digestive tract, which can help ease morning sickness symptoms.
Carminative: Fennel's volatile oils have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, reducing gas, bloating, and discomfort that often accompany morning sickness.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone, a monoterpene ketone, helps alleviate morning sickness by exhibiting antiemetic properties, reducing nausea and vomiting.
Anethole: Anethone, an aromatic ether, has been shown to have a sedative effect on the stomach, thereby reducing morning sickness symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Trans-anethol: Trans-anethol, a type of anethole, may also contribute to the antiemetic effects of Foeniculum vulgare by modulating gut motility and reducing the frequency and severity of morning sickness episodes.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are commonly used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly fenchone, which has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and other symptoms associated with morning sickness.
Leaves: The leaves of Fennel are used to make tinctures due to their mild anise-like flavor and aroma, which can help soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea.
Roots: The roots of Fennel are used to make tinctures due to their rich content of coumarin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare root and 2 parts of foeniculum vulgare leaves.
Combine the gathered parts with 70 percent ethanol in a ratio of 1:2.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill tinctures helps with morning sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can ease nausea and discomfort. The essential oils present in dill, such as carvone and limonene, have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, reducing symptoms of morning sickness. Additionally, dill's natural digestive benefits help to soothe an upset stomach and promote a sense of relief from morning sickness, allowing pregnant women to feel more comfortable throughout their day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dill's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and nausea associated with morning sickness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of dill, which include polyphenols and carotenoids, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and nausea during pregnancy.

Gastroprotective properties: Dill's gastroprotective compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and damage in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of morning sickness like queasiness and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Dill Terpenes: Dill terpenes, particularly carvone and limonene, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate morning sickness symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Phenolic Compounds (including Apiol): Phenolic compounds like apiol, a lignan, have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to prevent vomiting and alleviate nausea associated with morning sickness.

Furanocoumarins (including Bergapten): Furanocoumarins, such as bergapten, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce morning sickness symptoms by stabilizing the stomach and alleviating nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Dill seeds are commonly used in tinctures for morning sickness due to their high concentration of carvone, a compound that helps alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Leaves: Dill leaves are used in tinctures for their carvone content, which has been shown to have anti-nausea properties and help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
Flowers: Dill flowers are also used in tinctures for their carvone content, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried anethum graveolens flowers and leaves in large quantities according to herbalist's guidelines.
Mix one part of anethum graveolens with one part of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks to allow maceration.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and residue completely.
Transfer the clear liquid tincture to small dark glass bottles and seal them tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with morning sickness because it contains isoflavones, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. These phytoestrogens work by binding to estrogen receptors in the body, regulating hormonal imbalances that can contribute to morning sickness. Additionally, red clover's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe digestive upset and calm the stomach, providing relief from the discomforts associated with morning sickness.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tincture contains isoflavones, which have been shown to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms by reducing nausea and vomiting by binding to estrogen receptors in the body, promoting balance and relaxation.

Vitexin: Vitexin, a flavonoid compound found in red clover, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which may contribute to morning sickness symptoms.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover tincture, such as formononetin and biochanin A, have a mild estrogenic effect on the body, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may exacerbate morning sickness symptoms during pregnancy.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: This flavonoid compound helps with morning sickness by inhibiting the enzyme COX-2, which is involved in inflammation and nausea, thus reducing the severity of morning sickness symptoms.

Formononetin: This isoflavone compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate morning sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Genistein: This isoflavone compound has been found to have a positive effect on nausea and vomiting by interacting with the neurotransmitter serotonin, which plays a role in regulating nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Trifolium pratense contain isoflavones, which may help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of Trifolium pratense are rich in phytoestrogens, which may help stabilize hormone levels and reduce morning sickness.
Roots: The roots of Trifolium pratense contain a range of bioactive compounds, including isoflavones, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh trifolium pratense flowers and leaves on a sunny day at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 cup of dried trifolium pratense with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its ability to soothe and calm the stomach. The anti-inflammatory properties in licorice root help to reduce nausea and vomiting, while also promoting digestion and reducing acidity. Additionally, licorice tincture's antispasmodic effects can ease cramping and discomfort associated with morning sickness, providing relief for women experiencing severe symptoms during pregnancy. Its gentle yet effective nature makes it a popular natural remedy for alleviating morning sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in licorice root may help relax the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, which can help alleviate morning sickness symptoms such as cramping and spasms.
Adaptogenic property: Licorice root has been traditionally used to calm and adapt the body to stress, which may help reduce anxiety and emotional stress that can exacerbate morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate morning sickness symptoms by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These flavonoids have antioxidant properties that may help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness by protecting the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals.

Saponins (specifically, glycyrrhetic acid): Glycyrrhetic acid has antiemetic properties that may help alleviate morning sickness symptoms by blocking the action of neurotransmitters that trigger nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra in tinctures for morning sickness is the roots, which contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to alleviate nausea.
Leaves: The leaves are another part used to make tinctures for morning sickness, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-emetic properties.
Flowers: The flowers are also used to make tinctures for morning sickness, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce nausea and alleviate symptoms of morning sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of clean dried root material in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the root material into small pieces to increase the surface area for solvent absorption.
Combine the chopped root material with 40% ethanol solvent in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with morning sickness because it contains flavonoids, a type of antioxidant that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. The tannins present in the tree's leaves also have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce stomach acid and soothe digestive issues. Additionally, maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, which can be beneficial for women experiencing morning sickness during pregnancy. By taking a tincture made from the maidenhair tree, women may find relief from these unpleasant symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate nausea and vomiting symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: The tannins and flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba tinctures can help dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating symptoms of morning sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.

Gastroprotective properties: The terpenes and triterpenoids in Maidenhair Tree tinctures have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate morning sickness by acting as antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and stabilizing mood, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Bilobalide, a terpenoid found in Ginkgo biloba, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.

Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide B): Ginkgolide B, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate the symptoms of morning sickness, including nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are widely used in herbal remedies due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in tinctures to help alleviate morning sickness, as they contain a compound called ginkgotoxin, which is thought to have anti-nausea properties.

Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are sometimes used in herbal remedies to alleviate morning sickness, possibly due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature ginkgo biloba leaves and stems at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed electric grinder at 10000 rpm.
Combine the powdered plant material with 80% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight and steep for 2 weeks.
Filter the tincture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its natural ability to soothe the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort. The herb's sweet, citrusy flavor and calming properties work together to ease nausea and vomiting, providing relief for women experiencing morning sickness during pregnancy. Additionally, lemon verbena has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce stomach acid and cramping, further contributing to a smoother digestive process and reduced morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic property: Lemon verbena tincture has been shown to have antiemetic effects, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness by soothing the stomach and intestines.
Antispasmodic property: The tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms that can exacerbate morning sickness symptoms.

Anxiolytic property: Lemon verbena has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress related to morning sickness, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting, making them an effective ingredient in natural remedies for morning sickness.
Flowers: Containing volatile oils and flavonoids that provide calming and soothing properties, helping to reduce morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloysia citriodora leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold running water and pat dry with clean paper towels.
Chop 100g of fresh aloysia citriodora leaves into small pieces and combine with 500ml of high-proof vodka.
Steep the leaf-vodka mixture in a glass jar for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and transferring 20ml of the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with morning sickness because its active compound, berberine, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties may help combat the underlying infections or imbalances in the gut that can exacerbate morning sickness symptoms. The tincture's bitter flavor also stimulates digestion and can help reduce nausea and vomiting by promoting the elimination of toxins from the body.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound in Goldenseal, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.

Berberastatin A: This compound has been found to possess antihistaminic and anti-allergic activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of morning sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset.

Oleuropein: Oleuropein, another key component of Goldenseal, is a potent antioxidant that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to alleviating morning sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain berberine, a natural compound with anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of morning sickness.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the goldenseal plant are used to make medicinal tinctures as they contain a concentrated amount of berberine, which may help to soothe digestive issues associated with morning sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh goldenseal roots and clean them thoroughly with water and a soft brush.
Slice the roots into thin pieces and combine them with 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place, ready to use as needed at a dosage of 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines. As a natural demulcent, slippery elm forms a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and irritation caused by hormonal changes during pregnancy. This calming effect can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness, allowing women to feel more comfortable and relieved throughout their day. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce digestive discomfort and promote a sense of overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by morning sickness.

Soother of mucous membranes: The mucilages in slippery elm can help protect and soothe the mucous membranes lining the stomach, esophagus, and intestines, providing relief from the nausea, vomiting, and abdominal cramps associated with morning sickness.

Prebiotic properties: Slippery elm's prebiotic fibers can help promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, supporting a healthy digestive system and reducing symptoms of morning sickness such as bloating, cramping, and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with morning sickness.
Bark: The bark of the slippery elm plant contains mucilage, a natural demulcent that coats and protects the mucous membranes in the stomach, providing relief from nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and stems in early morning or late afternoon, ensuring at least 4 ounces of dried material.
Combine the harvested material with 80 proof vodka and fill a clean glass jar leaving one inch headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking the jar twice daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator for up to one year taking 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb's mucilages create a protective barrier in the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by nausea and vomiting. Additionally, marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with morning sickness. By calming the stomach and reducing inflammation, marshmallow tinctures provide relief from morning sickness, making it easier for pregnant women to manage this uncomfortable symptom.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine effect: Herbal marshmallow tincture contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antihistamine properties, helping to alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins present in marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of morning sickness like bloating and stomach discomfort.

Soothing mucilages: Marshmallow root contains a high concentration of mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, esophagus, and intestines, providing relief from the burning sensation and discomfort associated with morning sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe digestive issues and alleviate nausea symptoms.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with morning sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are dry.
Cleanse the plant material by gently shaking off any dirt or debris into a colander.
Chop 500 grams of the cleaned plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar leaving behind the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, relieving discomfort and cramping associated with morning sickness. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties can help counteract the oxidative stress caused by hormonal changes during pregnancy, further reducing the severity of morning sickness symptoms. As a result, incorporating herbal turmeric tinctures into one's daily routine may provide relief from morning sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with morning sickness.

Gastroprotective properties: Turmeric has natural gastroprotective compounds that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux and digestive enzymes, reducing symptoms of morning sickness like heartburn and bloating.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation that exacerbate morning sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, is used due to its high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of morning sickness.

Roots: Turmeric roots contain a wealth of bioactive compounds, including turmerones and sesquiterpenes, which can be extracted into tinctures to provide relief from nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots, wash them thoroughly, and chop them into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped turmeric root with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar, seal tightly.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and store the resulting turmeric tincture in a cool dark place, using 1/2 teaspoon twice daily to alleviate morning sickness symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries at peak ripeness for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested berries in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried vaccinium myrtillus berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Combine one part ground berries with four parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, steeping for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with morning sickness because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at dawn or mid-morning when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags or cloth for 7-10 days.
Grind 4 ounces of dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to enhance extraction efficiency.
Combine 1 part powdered sambucus nigra with 2 parts vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar, stirring well to ensure even mixture.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and decanting into amber glass bottles.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to morning sickness that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as nausea, vomiting, and bloating. Herbal remedies like Ginger (Zingiber officinale) and Peppermint (Mentha piperita) have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), motion sickness, and menstrual cramps. These tinctures work by calming the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and soothing the stomach.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with morning sickness is a blend of Ginger (Zingiber officinale), Peppermint (Mentha piperita), and Unani's Triphala. Ginger tincture aids digestion and reduces nausea, while Peppermint tincture helps alleviate stomach discomfort and cramps. Triphala, a blend of three herbs, supports gut health and balances digestive enzymes. Take 5-10 drops of each tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, to alleviate morning sickness symptoms. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies during pregnancy.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Australian & New Zealand journal of obstetrics & gynaecology, herbal tinctures, specifically ginger extract, can be beneficial for morning sickness due to their ability to reduce nausea and retching. In a double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled trial involving 120 pregnant women, a ginger extract (125 mg given four times per day) significantly reduced nausea and retching compared to a placebo. The results showed that the nausea experience score was less for the ginger extract group after the first day of treatment, and this difference persisted throughout the 4-day treatment period, making ginger a useful treatment option for women experiencing morning sickness.
